data.title: Nike Stock Slides on Tariff Impact Despite Earnings Beat; Turnaround Continues — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Nike fiscal third-quarter earnings are due after the closing bell Tuesday and management will be seeking to convince investors that the company's business turnaround is the real deal.

Wall Street expects the Beaverton, Ore.-based footwear and athletic apparel retailer to post fiscal third-quarter earnings of 29 cents per share, representing a 46.3% drop-off compared with a year ago, with revenue totaling $11.23 billion, which would mark a 0.3% decline year-over-year, according to FactSet.

A team of Jefferies analysts, led by Randal Konik, on Monday wrote they expect fiscal third-quarter performance to be driven by sales in North America and in Nike's wholesale segment. However, the analysts believe the China market and direct-to-consumer sales "remain under pressure."

The firm added that Nike is progressing in turning around its business and that momentum is building in North America.

The third-quarter earnings results come at 4:15 p.m. Eastern time on Tuesday. Company executives are scheduled to hold the conference call at 5 p.m. ET.

During the conference call, investors are expected to be looking to see if the company changes course and decides to provide full-year guidance. Nike has refrained from offering a full-year outlook for several quarters now as it works on its business turnaround.

Recent investor focus has been on Nike leadership and CEO Elliott Hill, who took the job in October 2024, on improving the company's business direction and on maintaining stable growth.

However, the market has been unconvinced thus far by Hill and Nike.

Nike stock advanced 1.7% to $52.10 on Tuesday ahead of the earnings report. For comparison, the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 1.3% and 0.9%, respectively.

On Monday, Nike closed at $50.98, it's lowest closing price since Oct. 13, 2017, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Entering Tuesday, Nike shares were down 17.59% in March, part of a 19.57% decline on the year.

In December 2025, Nike surpassed Wall Street predictions for both fiscal second-quarter earnings and revenue, but investors were disappointed over declining profits and lackluster sales in China.

Nike's third-quarter guidance also did not sit well with investors, with the footwear and athletic apparel retailer forecasting modest growth in North America with trends in Greater China and Converse in line with the second quarter.

Nike also expects gross margins to be down between 1.75 and 2.25 percentage points from a year ago.

"Over the past several quarters, Nike has struggled significantly, amid a confluence of unfavorable external and internal hindrances, which have weighed meaningfully upon sales growth and profit expansion," Oppenheimer analysts Brian Nagel and Andrew Chasanoff wrote Friday.

The analysts added that challenges persist for Nike but the current share price indicates "downbeat market sentiment," which could lead to "favorable re-rating as fundamental trends at the company re-strengthen and start to consistently top Street forecasts."

Oppenheimer has a price target of $120 on Nike, along with a "top pick" designation in its "consumer growth & ecommerce" coverage group. The firm has an Outperform rating on Nike.

It appears Nike's turnaround is far from over. In a March 5 regulatory filing, Nike said it expects to take a pretax charge of about $300 million, mostly in the third quarter, because of employee severance costs over a nine month period ending Feb. 28. The company said the move comes as management continues to evaluate opportunities to operate more efficiently and profitably.

However, the Jefferies analysts wrote that Nike's business is tracking in the right direction and that the upcoming FIFA World Cup is a clear global catalyst.

"We advise buying shares now as the turnaround advances," Konik wrote. Jefferies has a Buy rating on the stock with a $110 price target.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: S&P lifts Cook Islands credit rating as tourism boosts finances
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings raised the Cook Islands' long-term sovereign credit rating to BB- from B+ late on Tuesday, citing a tourism-driven economic rebound and fiscal prudence that has strengthened government finances.

It assigned a stable outlook to the rating and affirmed the Pacific island nation's short-term sovereign rating at B and kept its transfer and convertibility assessment at AAA.

S&P said the upgrade reflected "buoyant" economic conditions helped by strong tourist arrivals and government spending coming in below budget, particularly on wages and capital expenditure.

Although S&P expects a small fiscal deficit in the current year, reflecting the withdrawal of budget support from New Zealand and higher capital spending, it said stronger-than-expected tourism income and reprioritised expenditure should cushion the impact in later years.

The agency expects Cook Islands' net debt to continue falling after 2026, remaining around 15% to 20% of GDP over the following three years. Net debt dropped to 16.4% of GDP in fiscal 2025 from a peak of 37.6% in fiscal 2022, aided by rising tourism revenue and concessional donor financing.

Still, the agency said the rating remained constrained by the Cook Islands' narrow economic base, weak statistical capacity, lack of independent monetary policy and exposure to external shocks, including higher fuel costs and strains in relations with New Zealand.

data.title: McCormick 10-Q: Revenue $1,873.9M, EPS $3.77
data.description:
McCormick reported first-quarter net sales of $1,873.9 million and diluted earnings per share of $3.77, results driven largely by the recent McCormick de Mexico acquisition and a one-time remeasurement gain. Adjusted metrics exclude special charges and the remeasurement gain, showing adjusted diluted EPS of $0.66 and adjusted operating income of $267.6 million. The company noted modest organic growth alongside acquisition, pricing and foreign-exchange contributions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $1,873.9 million, up 16.7% versus prior year (acquisition +12.4%, pricing +1.9%, FX +3.1%, organic +1.2%).
  • Gross profit: $708.9 million; gross margin 37.8%; adjusted gross margin 38.6% (excluding special charges).
  • Operating income: $227.5 million; adjusted operating income $267.6 million (excluding special charges).
  • Net income: $1,022.3 million, which includes an $866.8 million gain on remeasurement of a previously held equity interest.
  • Earnings per share – diluted: $3.77; adjusted diluted EPS $0.66 (excluding the remeasurement gain and special charges).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and drivers: Revenue growth was driven primarily by the acquisition of McCormick de Mexico (~12.4% contribution); organic growth was approximately 1.2%.
  • Channel and segment performance: Consumer segment experienced an unfavorable volume/mix impact (-0.4%); Flavor Solutions saw a -1.0% mix headwind, partially offset by pricing gains.
  • Pricing and marketing: Consumer pricing increased ~2.2%; marketing investments increased in the mid-teens to support brand momentum.
  • Operational efficiency: The CCI program delivered cost savings and helped margin recovery, with adjusted gross margin improving roughly 100 basis points excluding special charges.
  • Strategic moves: Increased ownership to 75% of McCormick de Mexico and signed a merger agreement to combine with Unilever Foods, positioning the company for Latin America expansion and greater scale.

Original SEC Filing: MCCORMICK & CO INC [ MKC ] - 10-Q - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Exxon Mobil Files 8K - Other Events >XOM
data.description:
Exxon Mobil Corp. (XOM) filed a Form 8K - Other Events - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 31, 2026.

On March 26, 2026, Exxon Mobil Corporation (the "Company") entered into an underwriting agreement (the "Underwriting Agreement") with RBC Capital Markets, LLC, J.P. Morgan Securities LLC and UBS Securities LLC, as managers of the several underwriters named therein, for the issuance and sale by the Company of $169,312,000 aggregate principal amount of its Floating Rate Notes due 2076 (the "Notes").

The Notes were issued pursuant to an indenture entered into by the Company on March 20, 2014 with Deutsche Bank Trust Company Americas, as trustee (the "Trustee"), as supplemented and amended by a first supplemental indenture dated as of June 26, 2020 between the Company and the Trustee, and as further supplemented by an officer's certificate dated March 30, 2026 establishing the terms and forms of the Notes.

The Notes were offered pursuant to the Company's Registration Statement on Form S-3 filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on February 18, 2026 (Reg. No. 333-293558) (the "Registration Statement").

The Underwriting Agreement and the Officer's Certificate (including the forms of the Notes) are filed as Exhibits 1.1 and 4.3, respectively, to this current report on Form 8-K and are incorporated by reference into the Registration Statement. The opinions of Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP and Timothy Kim, Esq., Counsel—Corporate of Exxon Mobil Corporation, are filed as Exhibits 5.1 and 5.2, respectively, to this current report on Form 8-K and are incorporated by reference into the Registration Statement.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/34088/000119312526134838/d121881d8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/34088/000119312526134838/0001193125-26-134838-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Beyond Meat Logs Lower 4Q Sales, Will Miss Annual Report Filing Deadline
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Beyond Meat posted a drop in fourth-quarter sales and said it won't file its annual report on time after discovering more accounting problems.

The plant-based protein company on Tuesday posted a profit of $409.9 million, and a loss of 29 cents on a per share basis, in the quarter ended Dec. 31, compared with a loss of $44.9 million, or 65 cents a share, a year earlier.

Beyond Meat said it recorded a $548.7 million non-cash gain due to a debt restructuring, which bolstered income in the quarter.

Revenue fell 20% to $61.6 million. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $63 million. Weak category demand and lower sales of chicken and burger products to restaurant customers weighed on the total volume of products sold.

"Our results for the fourth quarter of 2025 reflect ongoing headwinds in the plant-based meat category as well as the financial impact of several restructuring charges," Chief Executive Ethan Brown said.

Beyond Meat delayed its earnings report at least twice in March, saying it identified errors in previously issued financial statements.

On Tuesday, the company said it found a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting in addition to the weakness already disclosed. The newly disclosed issue relates to Beyond Meat's accounting for inventory provision.

Beyond Meat said it is unable to file its annual report by the deadline because it needs more time to complete its fourth-quarter and year-end financial close procedures. It can't estimate when the form will be filed, it said.

The company said it expects first-quarter revenue of $57 million to $59 million, which would be below the $65.6 million analysts are projecting.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: TXNM Energy convertible notes become exercisable; conversion rate ~22.5382 shares/$1,000, conversion price ~$44.37
data.description:
TXNM Energy announced its 5.75% Junior Subordinated Convertible Notes due 2054 are convertible April 1–June 30, 2026 at 22.5382 shares per $1,000 (≈$44.37/share).

Key Highlights:

  • Convertible Notes convertible during Second Quarter 2026 Conversion Period: April 1–June 30, 2026; conversion rate 22.5382 shares per $1,000 principal.
  • On conversion, TXNM will issue equal principal amount of new 5.75% non-convertible junior subordinated notes due 2054 for conversion obligation up to principal amount.
  • Conversion obligation in excess of principal will be settled in common stock (plus cash for fractions) based on a 60-trading-day observation period per indenture.
  • Merger agreement could change conversion rights—if a make-whole fundamental change occurs, converting holders would receive cash equal to conversion rate × merger price.

Original SEC Filing: TXNM ENERGY INC [ TXNM ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

data.title: Seritage Growth reports $18.2M revenue, $73.1M annual net loss and $62.3M year-end cash
data.description:
Seritage Growth reported results for the year ended December 31, 2025, showing total revenue of $18.2 million and a net loss attributable to common shareholders of $73.1 million (or $1.30 per share). The company generated $230.7 million of total gross proceeds from asset sales during 2025, repaid $190.0 million of term loan principal and held $62.3 million in cash at year-end. Management disclosed continued execution of its Plan of Sale, one property under contract post-year-end, and substantial doubt about the company’s ability to continue as a going concern given the term loan maturity in July 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025: $18.2 million (rental income $17.6 million; management and other fee income $0.6 million).
  • Net loss attributable to Seritage Growth common shareholders for year: $73.1 million; net loss per share: $1.30.
  • Impairment of real estate assets recorded for the year: $18.8 million.
  • Cash on hand as of December 31, 2025: $62.3 million (including $14.2 million restricted cash); cash on hand as of March 31, 2026: $59.1 million (including $14.3 million restricted).
  • Term loan facility outstanding principal reduced to $50.0 million at December 31, 2025 after $190.0 million in principal repayments during 2025; term loan maturity noted as July 31, 2026.

Business Highlights

  • Generated $230.7 million of total gross proceeds from asset sales in 2025, including three major disposals in Q4 (vacant asset, income-producing asset at 7.4% cap rate, and a non-stabilized premier income-producing property).
  • Subsequent to year-end received a $5.7 million distribution from an unconsolidated entity following a sale of underlying property and have one consolidated asset under contract for anticipated gross proceeds of $11.0 million subject to customary conditions.
  • Invested $26.3 million in consolidated properties during 2025, primarily tenant leasing costs, and invested $0.5 million in unconsolidated properties; received $11.3 million in distributions from unconsolidated properties for the year.
  • Portfolio as of December 31, 2025 consisted of interests in 10 properties totaling ~0.8 million square feet of GLA and 156 acres (five consolidated properties ~0.3M GLA / 71 acres and five unconsolidated entities ~0.5M GLA / 85 acres).
  • Company continues strategic review under its Plan of Sale, is marketing remaining assets, and is exploring financing alternatives and potential strategic transactions as it simplifies the portfolio.

Original SEC Filing: Seritage Growth Properties [ SRG ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Oric Pharmaceuticals presents dose optimization data for rinzimetostat, selects 400 mg QD provisional Phase 3 dose
data.description:
Oric Pharmaceuticals presented dose-optimization data showing 400 mg QD rinzimetostat plus darolutamide selected as the provisional recommended Phase 3 dose with favorable tolerability and promising early efficacy signals.

Key Highlights:

  • Presented Phase 1b dose-optimization results for rinzimetostat (ORIC-944) in combination with darolutamide on March 31, 2026.
  • Exposure-response analyses supported 400 mg QD as the RP3D due to lower toxicity and fewer dose modifications vs 600 mg QD with comparable efficacy signals.
  • 400 mg QD + darolutamide showed high ctDNA MR50 rate (71%) and confirmed PSA50 in 33% of evaluable post-abiraterone mCRPC patients.
  • Safety profile at 400 mg QD was generally well tolerated: mostly Grade 1–2 TRAEs, low Grade ≥3 incidence (6% in post-abiraterone cohort) and no Grade 4/5 in that subgroup.
  • First global Phase 3 trial (Himalayas-1) in post-abiraterone mCRPC planned to start in 1H 2026, targeting rPFS with ~300 patients per arm.

Original SEC Filing: Oric Pharmaceuticals, Inc. [ ORIC ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Jamie Dimon Says Success in Iran Is 'Much More Important' Than What the Market Does — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

JPMorgan Chase's Jamie Dimon said a successful completion to the conflict in Iran is "much more important" than what the stock market is doing in the short term.

Talking about the now 32-day war in Iran on Fox & Friends, the CEO of the biggest U.S. bank and a prominent voice on Wall Street said: "It's much more important that this be successfully completed, than what the market does."

Stocks surged in early afternoon trading, with the S&P 500 up 2.6% and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite up 3.6%. The Dow added nearly 1,000 points, or 2.2%.

President Donald Trump has sent more U.S. military forces to the Middle East and talked about a possible ground invasion. U.S. gas prices have climbed to an average of $4 a gallon for the first time since 2022 as oil's price rises.

But on Tuesday, Trump reportedly told aides he would be willing to end the war even if the U.S. and its allies failed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And in the early afternoon, Bloomberg TV put up a headline that said "Iran President Says Ready to End War: IRNA."

Dimon told the Fox show that markets are unpredictable, but could be reflecting worries that there's a chance something could go wrong. "Now we should all hope nothing goes wrong. We should all hope that these bad people are, you know, that we win this thing and clean up the straits and that Iran is no longer a threat to everybody, and but there are, the markets will be concerned until it's over."

To those who insist Iran did not pose an imminent threat to the U.S., Dimon said Iran has been "doing something bad for 47 years," including killing Americans and supporting proxy wars.

"I think people were surprised to find out they had a ballistic missile that could go 3,000 miles," he said. "These are bad people and they needed to be stopped."

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

BOSTON, March 31, 2026 /PRNewswire/ - John Hancock Tax-Advantaged Dividend Income Fund (the "Fund"), a closed-end fund managed by John Hancock Investment Management LLC and subadvised by Manulife Investment Management (US) LLC, announced today sources of its monthly distribution of $0.1580 per share paid to all shareholders of record as of March 12, 2026, pursuant to the Fund's managed distribution plan. This press release is issued as required by an exemptive order granted to the Fund by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Notification of Sources of Distribution

This notice provides shareholders of the John Hancock Tax-Advantaged Dividend Income Fund with important information concerning the distribution declared on March 2, 2026, and payable on March 31, 2026. No action is required on your part.

 
Distribution Period:                    March 2026 
Distribution Amount Per Common Share:   $0.1580

The following table sets forth the estimated sources of the current distribution, payable March 31, 2026, and the cumulative distributions paid this fiscal year to date from the following sources: net investment income; net realized short term capital gains; net realized long term capital gains; and return of capital or other capital source. All amounts are expressed on a per common share basis and as a percentage of the distribution amount.

You should not draw any conclusions about the Fund's investment performance from the amount of this distribution or from the terms of the Fund's managed distribution plan.

The Fund estimates that it has distributed more than its income and net realized capital gains; therefore, a portion of your distribution may be a return of capital. A return of capital may occur, for example, when some or all of the money that you invested in the Fund is paid back to you. A return of capital distribution does not necessarily reflect the Fund's investment performance and should not be confused with "yield" or "income."

The amounts and sources of distributions reported in this Notice are only estimates and are not being provided for tax reporting purposes. The actual amounts and sources of the amounts for tax reporting purposes will depend upon the Fund's investment experience during the remainder of its fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. The Fund will send you a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year that will tell you how to report these distributions for federal income tax purposes.

The Fund has declared the March 2026 distribution pursuant to the Fund's managed distribution plan (the "Plan"). Under the Plan, the Fund makes fixed monthly distributions in the amount of $0.1580 per share, which will continue to be paid monthly until further notice.

If you have questions or need additional information, please contact your financial professional or call the Manulife John Hancock Closed-End Fund Information Line at 1-800-843-0090, Monday through Friday between 8:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m., Eastern Time.

Statements in this press release that are not historical facts are forward-looking statements as defined by the United States securities laws. You should exercise caution in interpreting and relying on forward-looking statements because they are subject to uncertainties and other factors which are, in some cases, beyond the Fund's control and could cause actual results to differ materially from those set forth in the forward-looking statements.

An investor should consider a Fund's investment objectives, risks, charges and expenses carefully before investing.

About Manulife John Hancock Investments

We serve investors through a unique multimanager approach, complementing our extensive in-house capabilities with an unrivaled network of specialized asset managers, backed by some of the most rigorous investment oversight in the industry. The result is a diverse lineup of time-tested investments from a premier asset manager with a heritage of financial stewardship.

About Manulife Wealth & Asset Management

As part of Manulife Financial Corporation, Manulife Wealth & Asset Management provides global investment, financial advice, and retirement plan services to 19 million individuals, institutions, and retirement plan members worldwide. Our mission is to make decisions easier and lives better by empowering people today to invest for a better tomorrow. As a committed partner to our clients and as a responsible steward of investor capital, we offer a heritage of risk management, deep expertise across public and private markets, and comprehensive retirement plan services. We seek to provide better investment and impact outcomes and to help people confidently save and invest for a more secure financial future. Not all offerings are available in all jurisdictions. For additional information, please visit manulifeim.com.

 
                    Gordon Haight

You should not draw any conclusions about the Fund's investment performance from the amount of this distribution or from the terms of the Fund's managed distribution plan.

The Fund estimates that it has distributed more than its income and net realized capital gains; therefore, a portion of your distribution may be a return of capital. A return of capital may occur, for example, when some or all of the money that you invested in the Fund is paid back to you. A return of capital distribution does not necessarily reflect the Fund's investment performance and should not be confused with "yield" or "income."

The amounts and sources of distributions reported in this Notice are only estimates and are not being provided for tax reporting purposes. The actual amounts and sources of the amounts for tax reporting purposes will depend upon the Fund's investment experience during the remainder of its fiscal year and may be subject to changes based on tax regulations. The Fund will send you a Form 1099-DIV for the calendar year that will tell you how to report these distributions for federal income tax purposes.

The Fund has declared the March 2026 distribution pursuant to the Fund's managed distribution plan (the "Plan"). Under the Plan, the Fund makes fixed quarterly distributions in the amount of $0.6500 per share.

If you have questions or need additional information, please contact your financial professional or call the Manulife John Hancock Closed-End Fund Information Line at 1-800-843-0090, Monday through Friday between 8:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m., Eastern Time.

Statements in this press release that are not historical facts are forward-looking statements as defined by the United States securities laws. You should exercise caution in interpreting and relying on forward-looking statements because they are subject to uncertainties and other factors which are, in some cases, beyond the Fund's control and could cause actual results to differ materially from those set forth in the forward-looking statements.

An investor should consider a Fund's investment objectives, risks, charges and expenses carefully before investing.

About Manulife John Hancock Investments

We serve investors through a unique multimanager approach, complementing our extensive in-house capabilities with an unrivaled network of specialized asset managers, backed by some of the most rigorous investment oversight in the industry. The result is a diverse lineup of time-tested investments from a premier asset manager with a heritage of financial stewardship.

About Manulife Investment Management

Manulife Investment Management is the global brand for the global wealth and asset management segment of Manulife Financial Corporation. We draw on more than a century of financial stewardship and the full resources of our parent company to serve individuals, institutions, and retirement plan members worldwide. Headquartered in Toronto, our leading capabilities in public and private markets are strengthened by an investment footprint that spans 18 geographies. We complement these capabilities by providing access to a network of unaffiliated asset managers from around the world. We're committed to investing responsibly across our businesses. We develop innovative global frameworks for sustainable investing, collaboratively engage with companies in our securities portfolios, and maintain a high standard of stewardship where we own and operate assets, and we believe in supporting financial well-being through our workplace retirement plans. Today, plan sponsors around the world rely on our retirement plan administration and investment expertise to help their employees plan for, save for, and live a better retirement. Not all offerings are available in all jurisdictions. For additional information, please visit manulife.com.

data.title: Radnostix reports $13.07M revenue, $0.91M net loss in 10-K
data.description:
Radnostix reported total revenues of $13.07 million for the year, down 6% from $13.90 million a year earlier, and recorded a net loss of $908,002; operating results reflected an operating loss of $956,187 while gross margin declined to 59% from 62% in the prior year. The filing notes the company omitted net income per share from Part 2 Item 7/8 per the provided instructions. These results reflect mixed segment momentum and operational actions taken during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $13.07 million (6% decrease vs. 2024 — $13.90M)
  • Gross Profit: $7.70 million; gross profit margin 59% (down from 62% in 2024)
  • Operating Income: Operating loss of $956,187 (reported as $(0.96) million) vs. operating profit of $6,901 in 2024
  • Net Income: Net loss of $908,002 (reported as $(0.91) million) vs. net income of $8,574 in 2024
  • Net Income Per Share: Omitted from Part 2 Item 7/Item 8 per instructions

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and momentum: Theranostics core represented 52% of total revenue, with I‑131 sales driving growth and new customer wins.
  • Channel and product shifts: Calibration & Reference products grew 21% and expanded into PET standards; theranostics and cobalt product lines experienced declines due to supply outages.
  • Operational milestones: Completed Co‑60 hot cell window refurbishments and prepared the 1359 building fit‑out for final NRC licensing.
  • Medical devices development: Continued development and acquisition of AMICI assets with plans to launch RadVent and other devices in 2026.

Original SEC Filing: RADNOSTIX INC [ INIS ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: PEDEVCO 10-K: Revenue $45.8M, EPS $(2.25)
data.description:
PEDEVCO reported oil, gas and NGL revenues of $45.8 million for the year, up 16% from the prior period driven by higher volumes, while net loss was $10.4 million, or $(2.25) per share, reflecting merger-related expenses and other non-operating charges. Operating income swung to an operating loss of $7.9 million from a $4.7 million gain last year, largely due to merger costs and higher operating expenses. Depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion of $18.0 million and increased interest and tax expense also weighed on results.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Oil, gas and NGL sales of $45.8 million, a 16% increase versus prior year.
  • Operating income: $(7.9) million, a decline from $4.7 million in the prior year primarily due to merger-related expenses and higher operating costs.
  • Net (loss) income: $(10.4) million, compared with $12.3 million in the prior year; decrease driven by merger costs, interest expense, credit loss and tax expense.
  • Net (loss) income per share – Basic / Diluted: $(2.25), versus $2.76 in the prior year.
  • Depreciation, depletion, amortization and accretion: $18.0 million, contributing to higher operating expenses and lower operating income.

Business Highlights

  • Production expansion: Daily production increased 36% to 2,494 BOE/day, with approximately 303 Mboe of sales added in November–December 2025 following October mergers.
  • Asset base and reserves: Proved reserves expanded to 32.1 MMBoe (an increase of 14.0 MMBoe) with PV-10 of about $357.7 million, driven by acquisitions in the D-J and PRB basins.
  • Transaction and integration activity: Completed October 2025 mergers adding assets in the D-J, PRB and Permian; sold legacy operated wells to reduce operations and maintenance liabilities.
  • Operational optimization and 2026 plans: 2026 capital expenditure guidance of $16–20 million focused on gas-lift conversions, recompletions, and pump/compression projects intended to lower operating costs.
  • Revenue drivers: Production-led revenue growth (36% higher production) offset by adverse price variance on realized sales prices.

Original SEC Filing: PEDEVCO CORP [ PED ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: HeartCore Enterprises 2025 10-K: Revenue $9.0M, Net Income $5.49M
data.description:
HeartCore Enterprises reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $8.97 million, a 60.5% decline from the prior year, driven primarily by a reduction in noncash IPO consideration; the company recorded net income of $5.49 million largely reflecting a $9.68 million gain on discontinued operations. Gross profit fell to $3.15 million with gross margin contracting to 35.1%, and continuing operations showed an operating loss of $3.12 million. The filing does not provide an explicit per-share net income figure in the disclosed summaries.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $8.97 million, decreased 60.5% vs prior year (from $22.69 million), mainly due to lower noncash IPO consideration.
  • Gross Profit: $3.15 million, decreased 78.6% vs prior year (from $14.72 million); gross margin reduced to 35.1%.
  • Operating Income (Loss): Loss from continuing operations of $(3.12) million, compared with $(0.16) million in the prior year.
  • Net Income: $5.49 million, a swing from a net loss of $(5.21) million in the prior year, driven by a $9.68 million gain on discontinued operations.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not explicitly provided in the summarized Item 7/8 disclosures.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Total revenue fell approximately 60% year-over-year to $9.0 million in 2025, primarily reflecting reduced noncash IPO consideration versus 2024.
  • Channel Shift: The company completed a strategic pivot away from its Japan software business toward U.S.-focused GO IPO consulting, including the sale of HeartCore Japan in October 2025.
  • Service Mix & Momentum: GO IPO consulting is the core service offering, with 16 client engagements structured as fees plus warrants to support U.S. listings.
  • New Initiative: Established Higgs Field in October 2025 to expand digital securities consulting and to pursue licensed securities activities in Japan.
  • Cost & Efficiency Actions: Following the divestiture of the Japan business, Sigmaways reduced subcontracting and cut R&D and marketing expenditures to conserve cash.

Original SEC Filing: HeartCore Enterprises, Inc. [ HTCR ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: GoHealth 2025 10-K: Revenue $361.8M, EPS (20.17)
data.description:
GoHealth reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $361.8 million and a net loss per share of $20.17 as the company sharply curtailed Medicare Advantage enrollment activity; the results include substantial impairment charges that drove an operating loss of $412.9 million and a net loss of $497.8 million. Management attributes the revenue decline of roughly 54.7% year‑over‑year to a strategic scaling back of Medicare Advantage operations and recognizes $259.96 million of indefinite and long‑lived asset impairment charges. The filing outlines steps taken to reduce costs and reposition the business toward more diversified, year‑round consumer offerings.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues: $361.8 million, down $437.0 million (54.7%) versus 2024 due to scaled back Medicare Advantage activities.
  • Income (loss) from operations: $(412.9) million, reflecting impairment and reduced revenues.
  • Net income (loss): $(497.8) million, which includes $259.96 million of indefinite and long‑lived asset impairment charges.
  • Net income (loss) attributable to GoHealth: $(257.1) million, after accounting for noncontrolling interests of $(240.6) million.
  • Net income (loss) per share of Class A common stock — basic and diluted: $(20.17); weighted‑average shares: 12,940.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and model shift: Management intentionally scaled back Medicare Advantage enrollment activity, driving a roughly 55% decline in net revenue and prompting a shift in channel mix from non‑agency toward an agency model (non‑agency mix fell from 27% to 15%).
  • Product and brand initiatives: Launched GoHealth Protect in 2025 to broaden offerings beyond Medicare and to drive year‑round consumer engagement.
  • Operational efficiency actions: Implemented Encompass Express, deployed AI/automation, and executed workforce reductions and infrastructure cuts to lower costs and improve operational efficiency.

Original SEC Filing: GoHealth, Inc. [ GOCO ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Fast Casual Concepts 2025 10-K: $65,700 Revenue, $0.00 EPS
data.description:
Fast Casual Concepts reported $65,700 of revenue in 2025 from new digital marketing services and delivered a reported net income of $792, which combines $59,631 from continuing operations and a $(58,839) loss from discontinued operations; basic and diluted net income per share was $0.00 on 26,112,754 weighted average shares. Operating loss narrowed to $(30,846) in 2025 from $(43,098) in 2024, driven by the new revenue stream and changes in expenses. Other income (expense) declined to $90,477, reflecting a smaller gain on extinguishment of debt versus the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $65,700 in 2025 (first continuing-operations revenue; $0 in 2024).
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(30,846) in 2025, improved from $(43,098) in 2024.
  • Net Income: $792 for 2025 (includes $59,631 from continuing operations and $(58,839) from discontinued operations).
  • Net Income Per Share (Basic & Diluted): $0.00, based on 26,112,754 weighted average shares.
  • Other Income (Expenses): $90,477 in 2025, down from $119,884 in 2024 due to a lower gain on extinguishment of debt.

Business Highlights

  • Business model shift: Transitioned from specialty beverage and franchising activities to focus on digital marketing consulting following cessation and disposition of prior operations during 2024–2025.
  • New revenue stream: Began providing digital marketing services in 2025, generating the company's first continuing-operations revenue and indicating early market acceptance.
  • Operational streamlining: Disposed of and ceased discontinued operations to refocus resources on service offerings and simplify the company’s operating profile.

Original SEC Filing: FAST CASUAL CONCEPTS, INC. [ FCCI ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

data.title: DUOS 2025 10-K: Revenue $27.02M, EPS (loss) $0.64
data.description:
DUOS reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $27.02 million and a net loss per common share of $0.64, reflecting a substantial increase in revenues driven by AMA services and initial hosting/solutions income that boosted gross margin. Despite higher operating expenses, operating loss narrowed to $(9.76) million and net loss improved to $(9.84) million versus the prior year. The company attributes the revenue and margin gains primarily to recurring managed services and new hosting revenue streams.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $27.02 million, a 271% increase versus $7.28 million in 2024, driven by AMA and new hosting/solutions revenue.
  • Gross margin: $7.88 million, up from $0.47 million in 2024 (1,579% increase), reflecting AMA revenue with no associated cost.
  • Loss from operations: $(9.76) million, improved from $(10.98) million in 2024, as higher revenues were partly offset by increased operating expenses.
  • Net loss: $(9.84) million, improved from $(10.76) million in 2024, primarily due to increased AMA-related revenues.
  • Net loss per common share: $0.64, improved from $1.39 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift to services: Strategic pivot from legacy inspection sales toward recurring services including AMA management, hosting, and technology solutions.
  • Edge data center rollout: Began deploying modular Edge Data Centers, generating initial hosting revenue and ongoing build-outs to expand hosting capacity.
  • AI and technology integration: Continued investment in AI, automation, and tech solutions to enable managed services and improve operational efficiency.
  • Operational milestones: Executed AMA with New APR, recognized equity-related revenue, and ramped Technology Solutions procurement and deployment.
  • Outlook (operational): Ongoing builds of Edge Data Centers and expanded managed services are positioned to support recurring revenue growth and further shift the business toward service-based models.

Original SEC Filing: DUOS TECHNOLOGIES GROUP, INC. [ DUOT ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Blink Charging 2025 10-K — $103.52M Revenue; Net loss $83.39M
data.description:
Blink Charging reported fiscal 2025 total revenues of $103.52 million and a net loss attributable of $83.385 million, an improvement versus the prior year driven by lower goodwill impairment charges and cost reductions. Gross profit declined to $25.50 million while loss from operations narrowed to $(84.10) million as operating expenses fell. The filing highlights a shift toward recurring charging services even as product sales declined sharply.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $103.52 million, decreased $20.52 million (‑17%) versus 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $25.50 million, decreased $12.14 million (‑32%) versus 2024.
  • Loss From Operations: $(84.10) million, improved $119.09 million versus 2024.
  • Net Loss (attributable): $(83.385) million, improved $117.933 million (‑59%) versus 2024.
  • Net Loss Per Share: Not separately stated as EPS in the provided section; net loss attributable is $(83.385) million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Product sales declined about 43% while charging services and network fees grew 51% and 53%, indicating a move toward recurring revenue streams.
  • Network scale & utilization: Approximately 66,350 chargers on Blink networks with increased utilization contributing to higher charging service revenue.
  • Operational restructuring: The BlinkForward program reduced workforce by roughly 38% and transitioned to contract manufacturing (completed January 2026) to lower overhead.
  • Strategic M&A and leadership moves: Acquired Zemetric in July 2025 and settled Envoy merger consideration while integrating fleet technology and appointing a new CTO.
  • Service and product focus: Expanded deployments of DC fast-charge solutions, launched the Shasta Level 2 platform, and emphasized energy and fleet management offerings.

Original SEC Filing: Blink Charging Co. [ BLNK ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

KULR Technology Group Inc. (KULR) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 31, 2026.

On March 31, 2026, KULR Technology Group, Inc. (the "Company") issued a press release announcing certain financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025. A copy of the press release is incorporated herein by reference and is furnished as Exhibit 99.1.

The information under this Item 2.02 is being furnished and shall not be deemed to be "filed" for the purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to the liabilities of such section, nor shall such information be deemed incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), or the Exchange Act, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1662684/000110465926037754/tm2610774d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1662684/000110465926037754/0001104659-26-037754-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Duos Technologies Q4 revenue surges
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. modular data center provider's Q4 revenue surged 548% yr/yr, driven by AMA with New APR

  • Net loss for Q4 narrowed 6% yr/yr as higher revenue offset increased operating expenses

Outlook

  • Duos expects 2026 revenue to exceed $50 mln, with most recognized in the second half

  • Company sees growth in services revenue from hosting and technology solutions in 2026

  • Duos says demand for AI and data center offerings remains strong, supporting backlog and pipeline

Result Drivers

  • AMA WITH NEW APR - Execution of the AMA with New APR drove substantial services revenue and improved gross margins in Q4

  • EDGE DATA CENTER HOSTING - Company began recognizing hosting revenue from Edge Data Centers, though scale remains limited

  • OPERATING EXPENSES - Higher operating expenses in Q4 were mainly due to non-cash stock-based compensation and one-time personnel costs

data.title: Nike 3Q Profit Declines as Weak Demand in China Drags On — Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Nike logged lower profit in its latest quarter as sales in China continued to decline, posing an obstacle for the company's turnaround strategy.

The sneaker and athletic apparel company on Tuesday posted a fiscal third-quarter profit of $520 million, or 35 cents a share, compared with $794 million, or 54 cents a share, a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet forecast earnings of 29 cents a share.

Revenue was roughly flat at $11.28 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $11.23 billion.

Wholesale revenue rose 5% to $6.5 billion, while direct revenue fell 4% to $4.5 billion.

Sales ticked up in North America as well as in the Europe, Middle East and Africa region and in the Asia Pacific and Latin America market. Sales slid 7%, though, in China.

The results come as Nike looks to stoke a turnaround, including efforts to rebuild its wholesale channel, grow its running business, clear out old inventory and refocus on the performance sportswear it's known for.

Chief Executive Elliott Hill said the pace of progress is different across Nike's portfolio, and the areas the company prioritized first continue to drive its momentum. The company said its turnaround efforts will continue to affect results this calendar year.

"The work is not finished, but the direction is clear, our teams are moving with focus and urgency, and our foundation is getting even stronger to build the future of Nike," Hill said.

Shares fell 2.7%, to $51.38, in after-hours trading. Through market close, the stock has fallen 17% over the past year.

The company's turnaround strategy, dubbed Win Now, has hit some snags in recent months. Continued weak demand in China, tepid growth in North America and added tariff costs posed further hurdles.

Nike has also been looking to trim costs lately, saying earlier this month it expects to record $300 million in pre-tax charges due to cost-cutting efforts, including job eliminations. The company said earlier this year it would lay off about 775 workers, primarily at distribution centers in Tennessee and Mississippi, as it ramps up its use of advanced technology and automation.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com
